human_verification.notes: Reviewed validated readiness for construction estimating workflow specificity, permit and inspection boundaries, estimate evidence handling, functionality coverage, and runtime-specification-based posture.

specialist_owned_evidence: Manifest keeps the lane specific to estimating execution, estimate basis development, quantity and quote traceability, revision handling, and completion evidence instead of generic finance or project controls.
Authoritative sources cover permit and inspection guidance, site-safety and changed-condition handling, and recognized estimating or construction-administration workflow references.
Functionality map covers all required shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and construction-and-field-services abilities with scenario coverage for every scenario in evaluation results.

Research summary records workflow stages, artifacts, systems of record, decision boundaries, domain failure modes, scenario-family coverage, and non-blocking ambiguities tied to tenant-supplied facts.

review_notes: No functional or evidence blockers remain for this package draft.

All required scenario families and queue-level must-pass families are represented, including permit-readiness gaps, closeout blockers, licensed-trade boundary refusal, changed-condition rework, and refusal to mark work complete without evidence.

notes: validated conditions met for a recommendation-safe construction and field-services estimating specialist spec pack.
Runtime remains specification-based and must be promoted to a retained implementation only through separate governance, usage evidence, and a dedicated commit or PR.
